title: Quantinuum Said to Weigh Boosting IPO Size and Price Range
summary: Honeywell International Inc.-backed quantum computing company Quantinuum Inc. is considering increasing the size of its initial public offering, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/30/2026
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Fmr. CDC Director Dr. Tom Frieden, The New York Times Texas Bureau Chief David Goodman and Cisco President and Chief Product Officer Jeetu Patel. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: This Millennial University President Sees Running Grambling State Like an Enterprise
summary: Dr. Martin Lemelle, Jr., Grambling State University President says he views running his HBU like an enterprise (Source: Bloomberg)

title: At Scrabble’s Biggest Tournament, Thailand Punches Above Its Weight
summary: Known locally as Crossword, Scrabble took off in Thailand in the 1980s after schools embraced it as a teaching aid and pathway to advancement. Much like athletic scholarships in the US, strong players can secure financial assistance and preferential admission to top universities, creating a pipeline of talent for Thailand’s competitive Scrabble circuit and helping produce a string of world champions. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Can AI Grow Without Hurting Local Communities?
summary: The rush to build AI data centers is drawing trillions of dollars in investment and long-term bets from infrastructure firms such as DigitalBridge. But in communities where those facilities are being built, residents and officials are raising concerns about electricity costs, water use, noise, transparency and who bears the risk if demand falls short. Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel is challenging approval of a major data center project near Ann Arbor, arguing that ratepayers deserve more transparency about contracts and potential costs. DigitalBridge CEO Marc Ganzi says the industry can navigate the backlash, but only by working with local communities and showing how the benefits outweigh the burdens. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ebola Outbreak Raises Global Health Concerns
summary: Travel restrictions and border closures are hindering the response to Ebola as aid workers say they are battling to get to some of the areas where the outbreak has been the most vicious. Former Center of Disease Control and Prevention Director Dr. Tom Frieden joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Reaffirms US Commitment to Pacific Allies
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. Bloomberg News Global Defense Editor Gerry Doyle joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the comments Hegseth made Saturday at an Asia security forum in Singapore, which are the latest sign of the Trump administration shifting attention toward the Indo-Pacific while expressing antagonism toward Europe and the NATO alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BP’s High-Stakes Reboot Descended Into an Ugly Boardroom Drama
summary: When Albert Manifold was hired as chairman of BP Plc, he joked to friends the company needed a “gurrier” — Irish slang for a savvy and scrappy streetfighter — to help turn its fortunes around.

title: Analysis Finds Trump's Deregulation Plan Falls Flat
summary: A White House plan to rapidly repeal regulations has flopped, with just a handful of rules dumped via the expanded use of an administrative shortcut since a widespread review effort was ordered more than a year ago. Bloomberg Law Principal Legal Reporter Robert Iafolla joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘Dinosaur’ Tech Stocks Reborn as AI Fuels $1.7 Trillion Rally
summary: They were all stars of the dot-com era before fading into the background as the bubble burst and a new generation of tech darlings rose to the forefront. But Dell Technologies Inc., Nokia Oyj and Lenovo Group Ltd. are back with a vengeance thanks to the unrelenting artificial intelligence spending boom.

title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Transits Are Rising Thanks to US Help
summary: Shipowners are increasingly optimistic about a pickup in traffic through the Strait of Hormuz after more vessels left the waterway this week with the US providing information to aid those making the journey.

title: Hegseth Praises Asian Allies, Takes Swipe at NATO
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. Bloomberg's Singapore Editor-At-Large Haslinda Amin joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the comments Hegseth made Saturday at an Asia security forum in Singapore, which are the latest sign of the Trump administration shifting attention toward the Indo-Pacific while expressing antagonism toward Europe and the NATO alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Britain’s Bond Market Is Sounding the Alarm
summary:
As political uncertainty grows in the United Kingdom, investors are increasingly focused on the country’s fiscal outlook and rising government borrowing costs. Bloomberg Opinion Columnist John Authers says concerns over spending, debt, and political instability are pushing gilt yields higher, reviving memories of past market crises that helped topple governments from Harold Wilson to Liz Truss. With Britain’s debt burden elevated and both major parties under pressure to spend more, the bond market is increasingly shaping the limits of economic policy. Authers argues the UK may be confronting fiscal pressures earlier than other advanced economies, offering a warning about the broader risks facing heavily indebted governments worldwide.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Under Pressure, While Poland Surges Ahead
summary: It will take a long time to bring aluminum back.

title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Europe, US Arms Deals
summary: South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace is increasing weapons manufacturing at home, and expanding operations in Europe and the US. The company's Global Chief Strategy Officer, Alex Wong spoke with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Hails China Relationship at Defense Summit
summary:
Jabs at European allies and talks of stable US-China ties — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth took to the stage at a security summit in Singapore this weekend. 

But two topics were notably missing from his address: Taiwan and the Iran war. Haslinda Amin with more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Pledges to Retaliate If EU Imposes Trade Restrictions
summary: China warned it would “resolutely” retaliate if the European Union proceeds with new restrictive trade measures, following a May 29 European Commission discussion on China policy.

title: New Zealand’s Penk on Hormuz Uncertainty (Video) (Correct)
summary: New Zealand’s Defense Minister Chris Penk speaks to Bloomberg TV about how the Iran war is shaping his country’s security outlook, and the importance of freedom of navigation through the seas. He speaks with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Corrects name of interviewee.) (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qatar Says Temporary Hormuz Fee to Clear Mines Is Negotiable
summary: Qatar opposes permanent legal fees for transit through the Strait of Hormuz, but a temporary fee is negotiable and could help restore normal passage through the key waterway, a top official said.

title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Germany, UK Arms Deals as Demand Surges
summary: Hanwha Aerospace Co. is in talks with Germany, the UK and other European nations for new weapons deals, seeking to capitalize on a massive wave of global arms spending triggered by conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

title: Former Ambassador Cui On Stabilizing US-China Ties
summary: Cui Tiankai, a former Chinese ambassador to the US, explains how the US and China should work toward "constructive strategic stability." Cui, who's also a former vice minister of foreign affairs for China, speaks to Bloomberg on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: MiniMax Eyes China Listing, Takes on AI Rivals Like DeepSeek
summary: China’s MiniMax Group Inc. has begun preparations for a domestic listing, according to a regulatory filing, as the fast‑growing artificial intelligence startup challenges local rivals including DeepSeek.

title: Remembering the Life and Work of Jonathan Clements: Masters in Business
summary: Barry sits down with Jason Zweig and William Bernstein. They discuss "Money and Me" the last book of author and journalist Jonathan Clements. Jason and William also examine Clements's approach to personal finance and impact to financial journalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Malaysian Defense Minister Says Won’t Rush Spending
summary: Malaysian defense chief Mohamed Khaled Nordin told Bloomberg that the country will not rush to increase its defence budget despite US pressure for partners to become more self-reliant. Khaled spoke with Haslinda Amin in an exclusive interview at Asia’s premier defense summit, the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NATO Military Head Says No ‘Drama’ With US
summary: Europe has responded to President Donald Trump’s demands to pay more for defense, a top NATO military official said Saturday, adding that the relationship with the Pentagon is stable and free of “drama.” Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, chair of NATO’s Military Committee, spoke with Haslinda Amin at the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How the Invention of Rope Gave Us Modern Civilization | Odd Lots
summary: Rope is easy to take for granted. It seems obvious and straightforward. But of course, it had to be invented. Early humans discovered that by twisting fibers around each other, the resulting structure would be something durable and strong. Without rope, all kinds of things aren't possible, from lifting objects into the air to whaling or modern bridges. So how was rope developed and what were the big breakthroughs in its history? On this episode, we speak with Tim Queeney, the author of 'Rope: How a Bundle of Twisted Fibers Became the Backbone of Civilization.' He walks us through the history of the technology, and its ongoing evolution, including how it might one day allow us to build elevators into outer space. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EasyJet Draws Early Interest From Investment Firm Castlelake
summary: EasyJet Plc, the UK airline that helped pioneer ultra low-cost travel in Europe over the past three decades, has drawn takeover interest from an investment firm, potentially shaking up the regional airline market as it navigates high fuel costs and steep competition.

title: Philippine Central Bank Says Inflation May Surge Further in May
summary: The Philippine central bank said the pace of inflation is likely to increase this month, mainly because of food-price increases and the peso’s weakness.

title: Hegseth Takes Swipe at European Defense Partners
summary:
“Western Europe might take note.”

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hailed stable US ties with China and praised allies in Asia, while taking swipes at longstanding partners in Europe, during a speech at a defense forum in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)
